title: Add a CSS-only 3D tilt hover effect to the homepage movie cards
context: The movie card component is src/components/movie-card.tsx and the grid is src/components/movie-grid.tsx (recorded in JOURNAL.md by 004-movie-grid). Styling is Tailwind v4 (arbitrary values like [perspective:1000px] and [transform-style:preserve-3d] are available; a custom utility in globals.css is also acceptable if a class combination gets unwieldy). APPROACH — pure CSS, deliberately simple: give the card's wrapper a perspective, and on :hover apply a subtle fixed rotateX/rotateY tilt (a few degrees) with optional translateZ/scale lift and a deepened shadow, eased by a transform transition. A fixed tilt on hover is the whole scope — do NOT add JS mouse-position tracking, event handlers, a 'use client' directive, or any new npm dependency; the card must remain a server component. Keep the tilt tasteful (roughly 2-6deg) so text stays readable and neighboring cards aren't overlapped disruptively. Respect accessibility: gate the motion behind Tailwind's motion-reduce variant (or a prefers-reduced-motion media query) so reduced-motion users get no tilt. Toolchain is Bun.
goal: Hovering a movie card on the homepage smoothly tilts it in 3D (perspective + rotate transform with a depth cue like lift/shadow) and it returns to rest on mouse-out, implemented entirely with CSS — no JavaScript interactivity, no client components, no new dependencies — with lint, tests, and the production build all passing and the effect verified in the browser.
acceptance: The tilt is implemented in CSS only: src/components/movie-card.tsx (plus globals.css if used) gains perspective + hover rotate/translate transform classes with a transition, and contains no onMouseMove/onMouseEnter/onPointerMove handlers.
grep -l "use client" src/components/movie-card.tsx src/components/movie-grid.tsx returns no matches (both remain server components).
git diff HEAD -- package.json shows no added dependencies or devDependencies.
The effect is disabled under prefers-reduced-motion: the transform/transition classes use Tailwind's motion-reduce variant or an equivalent media query (show the code).
Run `bun test` from the app root — exits 0.
Run `bun run lint` from the app root — exits 0.
Run `bun run build` from the app root — exits 0.

Start the server on an open port in the background; via mcp__claude-in-chrome__ tools open the homepage, hover a movie card, and confirm it visibly tilts with 3D depth and returns to rest on mouse-out — capture a screenshot of the resting state and one mid-hover as proof.
Confirm the page produces no console errors while hovering across several cards.
Stop the background server after verification.
JOURNAL.md records the tilt implementation (classes/utility used, tilt angles, reduced-motion handling) for future tasks.
